---
title: "Sample Size"
description: "Compute the per-group sample size needed for a target power in a one-way ANOVA."
categories: [Experiments, "Experimental Design"]
---

::: {.callout-note title="Introduction"}
Use a power calculation to find how many observations per treatment you need to
detect a given effect size.
:::

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false
pacman::p_load(pwr)

# sigma2 carried over from the Conservative Estimate of Variance task
chi <- qchisq(.95, 36, lower.tail = FALSE)
sse <- 56.471
sigma2 <- sse/chi

delta <- 1.5                                            #given delta
v <- 4                                                  #treatment groups
#sigma2 from conservative estimate above
f <- sqrt((delta^2)/(2*v*sigma2))                       #effect size
pwr.anova.test(4,
               sig.level=.05,
               power = .95,                             #desired power
               f=f)
```

The recommended sample size from each treatment group is 39, with a total
sample size of 156.


## See also

- [Conservative Estimate of Variance](conservative-estimate-of-variance.qmd)
